title: Detect planner-provided shared followups in scheduler
description: Add scheduler logic that can recognize when the feature tree already contains explicit tester, documentation, or review beads whose dependencies include the completing developer bead, so those followups are treated as planner-owned.
acceptance_criteria: Scheduler code can inspect the feature tree for explicit tester/documentation/review beads related to a completed developer bead.
Detection is based on planner-provided beads that depend on the developer bead rather than on child ID suffixes alone.
The detection logic is isolated enough to support both shared feature-level followups and legacy per-bead auto-followups.

handoff_summary.completed: Implemented dependency-based followup detection in the scheduler, preserved legacy child followup lookup as a fallback, and wired auto-created review beads to depend on whichever tester/documentation followups already exist.
handoff_summary.remaining: No implementation work remains in this bead. Test execution was not run by design; tester/review agents can validate end-to-end behavior separately.
handoff_summary.risks: Planner-owned followups are recognized only when they explicitly list the completed developer bead in their dependencies. Plans that omit those dependencies will still fall back to legacy auto-followups.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Scheduler followup selection now prefers dependency-linked feature-tree beads over legacy child IDs for tester/documentation/review routing; downstream agents should account for shared followups being reused instead of recreated.

conflict_risks: Scheduler followup selection now prefers dependency-linked feature-tree beads over legacy child IDs for tester/documentation/review routing; downstream agents should account for shared followups being reused instead of recreated.

execution_history.summary: Updated scheduler followup detection so developer completion reuses explicit tester/documentation/review beads in the same feature tree when they already depend on the developer bead, instead of relying only on child suffix IDs.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
